revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大覚寺古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Maruyama Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu-1.jpg|location={{ill|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|1=Kyoto}}; {{ill|Ukyō Ward|qid=Q580186|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}}}}
'''Daikakuji Kofun Group''' (大覚寺古墳群) is a group of {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q580186|lt=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture, in the areas of Saga Daikakuji Monzen Noboricho, Saga Oosawa Yanagiidecho, and Saga Daikakuji Monzen Dounomaecho. Two of the kofun have been designated as {{ill|Imperial Candidate Imperial Mausoleums|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Candidate Imperial Mausoleums|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}. == Overview == This kofun group is situated in the western part of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|1=Kyoto Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10883263}} on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Arisugawa River|ja|有栖川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arisugawa River|1=Arisugawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q23997746}}. It comprises four kofun: three {{ill|circular tumuli (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumuli (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (1st, 3rd, and 4th kofun) and one {{ill|square tumulus (hōfun)|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus (hōfun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (2nd kofun). All kofun have undergone archaeological investigations. The largest kofun in the group is the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kyoto)|ja|円山古墳 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kyoto)|qq=}} (1st kofun), a large circular tumulus with a diameter of 50 meters. Its burial facility consists of a large {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with two assembled house-shaped Sarcophaguss made of Ryuzan stone, measuring 14.7 meters in total length. Grave goods such as {{ill|decorated swords|ja|装飾付大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorated swords|1=decorated swords|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q48749737}}, earrings, horse gear, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} were discovered inside the chamber. The other kofun, including the 2nd kofun (Nyudozuka Kofun), 3rd kofun (Nantenzuka Kofun), and {{ill|Kitsunezuka Kofun (Kyoto City)|lt=Kitsunezuka Kofun (Kyoto City)|lt=Kitsunezuka Kofun (Kyoto)|ja|狐塚古墳 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsunezuka Kofun (Kyoto)|qq=}} (4th kofun), are medium-sized circular or square tumuli, all with double-sleeved corridor-style stone chambers. The chambers of the 2nd and 3rd kofun contained fragments of house-shaped Sarcophaguss made of Ryuzan stone and decorated Sue pottery. Notably, Silla pottery was unearthed from the 3rd kofun. The construction period is estimated to span the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} to the {{ill|final stage of kofun|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/final stage of kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}, from the late 6th to early 7th centuries. The sequence of construction is believed to be 1st kofun → 3rd kofun → 4th kofun → 2nd kofun{{Sfn|Hanakuma Yuki|2016|qq=}}. The Daikakuji Kofun Group is one of the representative later-stage kofun groups in the Sagano region and is considered significant in studying the ancient history of the Uzumasa and Sagano areas, alongside nearby tumuli such as the {{ill|Hebizuka Kofun|en|Hebizuka Kofun|qid=Q11625508|lt=Hebizuka Kofun|1=Hebizuka Kofun}}. == Excavation History == * '''1899 (Meiji 32):''' The Imperial Household Ministry (now the Imperial Household Agency) designated the 1st and 2nd kofun as {{ill|Imperial Candidate Imperial Mausoleums|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Candidate Imperial Mausoleums|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}<ref>{{ill|Sotoike Noboru|ja|外池昇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotoike Noboru|1=Noboru Toike|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11430485}}, "Maruyama Imperial Candidate Imperial Mausoleum," 2005.</ref>. * '''1951 (Showa 26):''' Excavation of the stone chamber of the 1st kofun (reported in 2001 by {{ill|Kobayashi Yukio|ja|小林行雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobayashi Yukio|qq=}}){{Sfn|Shoryobu Memoirs Vol. 53|2001|qq=}}. * '''1958 (Showa 33):''' Excavation of the stone chamber of the 2nd kofun (reported in 2001){{Sfn|Shoryobu Memoirs Vol. 53|2001|qq=}}. * '''1970 (Showa 45):''' Survey and measurement of the mound and stone chamber of the 4th kofun (Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 1971). * '''1975 (Showa 50):''' Excavation of the 1st, 2nd, and 3rd kofun due to the construction of Kyoto Prefectural Kitano Saga High School (reported in 1976 by Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education). * '''2014-2016, 2018 (Heisei 26-28, 30):''' Survey and excavation of the 4th kofun mound (Ryukoku University, reported in 2016-2020). * '''2020 (Reiwa 2):''' Trial excavation around the 4th kofun (Kyoto City Cultural Property Division, reported in 2022){{Sfn|Kyoto City Excavation Report|2022|qq=}}. == List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Daikakuji Kofun Group List{{Sfn|Kyoto Archaeological Reports|1976|qq=}}{{Sfn|Shoryobu Memoirs Vol. 53|2001|qq=}}{{Sfn|Hanakuma Yuki|2016|qq=}} ! Kofun Name ! Shape ! Size ! Burial Facility ! Stone Chamber Size ! Grave Goods ! Construction Period ! Notes |- | '''{{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Kyoto)|ja|円山古墳 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kyoto)|qq=}}''' (1st kofun) || Circular || Diameter 50m || Double-sleeved corridor-style stone chamber (with two assembled house-shaped Sarcophaguss) || 14.7m || Decorated swords, earrings, horse gear, Sue pottery, Haji pottery || Late 6th century || {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designation: "Maruyama Imperial Candidate Imperial Mausoleum." |- | 2nd kofun (Nyudozuka Kofun) || Square || 25m × 30m || Double-sleeved corridor-style stone chamber (with an assembled house-shaped Sarcophagus) || Estimated 11m || Sue pottery (including decorated types) || Early 7th century || Designation: "Nyudozuka Imperial Candidate Imperial Mausoleum." |- | 3rd kofun (Nantenzuka Kofun) || Circular || || Double-sleeved corridor-style stone chamber (with an assembled house-shaped Sarcophagus) || 8.1m remaining || Gold earrings, Sue pottery, Silla pottery || Late 6th century || Currently buried; excavated in 1975. |- | '''{{ill|Kitsunezuka Kofun (Kyoto City)|lt=Kitsunezuka Kofun (Kyoto City)|lt=Kitsunezuka Kofun (Kyoto)|ja|狐塚古墳 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsunezuka Kofun (Kyoto)|qq=}}''' (4th kofun) || Circular || Diameter 28m || Double-sleeved corridor-style stone chamber || 6.8m remaining || Sue pottery || Early 7th century || Surveyed and excavated multiple times between 1970 and 2020. |} <gallery> Maruyama Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu-1.jpg|1st Kofun (Maruyama Kofun) Nyudozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|2nd Kofun (Nyudozuka Kofun) Kitsunezuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg|4th Kofun (Kitsunezuka Kofun) Daikakuji 3rd Kofun Silla Pottery.JPG|3rd Kofun (Nantenzuka Kofun) Silla Pottery </gallery> == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == References == {{small|Sources used in the article:|qq=}} * Published by the Imperial Household Agency ''' {{Cite book|ja|author={{ill|Kobayashi Yukio|ja|小林行雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobayashi Yukio|qq=}}|year=2001|chapter={{PDFlink|[https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp/Publication/PDF/000/kiyo053b001.pdf Maruyama and Nyudozuka Imperial Mausoleum Reports]|qq=}}|title=Shoryobu Memoirs Vol. 53|publisher=Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} * Published by Local Governments ''' {{Cite book|ja|author=|year=1976|chapter=Overview of Daikakuji Kofun Group Excavations|title=Kyoto Archaeological Reports|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|qq=}} ''' {{Cite book|ja|author=|year=2022|chapter=Daikakuji Kofun Group|title=Kyoto City Excavation Report 2021|publisher=Kyoto City Cultural Affairs Division|qq=}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|ja|author=Marukawa Yoshihiro|year=2002|chapter=Daikakuji Kofun Group|title=Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|qq=}} * Others ''' {{Cite journal|ja|author={{ill|Hanakuma Yuki|ja|花熊祐基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanakuma Yuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|title=Daikakuji 4th Kofun Excavation and Survey Report|date=2016|journal=Ryukoku University Archaeological Practices|publisher=Ryukoku University|qq=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Hebizuka Kofun|en|Hebizuka Kofun|qid=Q11625508|lt=Hebizuka Kofun|1=Hebizuka Kofun}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Daikakuji Kofun Group|qq=}}
